result:
I would eliminate the barrier of "otherness."

I would rewire the human capacity for empathy so that causing harm to another felt viscerally identical to harming oneself. If the pain of a stranger—or an enemy, or a future generation—resonated in your own nervous system as if it were your own, cruelty would not just be morally wrong; it would be biologically self-destructive. The world would shift instantly from a collection of warring tribes to a single, unified organism, not because laws forced it to, but because the illusion of separateness would simply no longer exist.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
